title: Potato Graphics
description: Give your game a hilarious low-end makeover! This pack turns every block into a single pixel, dramatically simplifying textures and boosting performance. Perfect for weak PCs or players who just want that ultimate potato graphics experience.
body:
![Completion](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/QUvmr7uI/images/383921510494cdc882535ae620346ad07a2ef543.png)

![Preview2](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/QUvmr7uI/images/54c3803bc8aa8283427138a45cf9b1d1b21a0585.png)


---

![Title](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/QUvmr7uI/images/2b8222fbd47dae61485f0114c83d71567f41fd8e.png)
## 🎮 Tired of lag? Want more FPS?

> The **Potato Resource Pack** is your new best friend! It **reduces Minecraft textures to the absolute minimum**, giving you a clean, absurd, potato-tier look — and a **performance boost**. 💨💻

> ✨ Perfect for **low-end PCs, laptops, toasters**, or anyone who wants Minecraft running as fast as possible.
> 🌍 Every block, every texture — boiled down to the bare minimum.

## 🧠 How does it work?

> In **default Minecraft**, most block textures are **16×16 pixels**, which equals **256 pixels per block**.

> 🥔 **Potato Pack pushes this to the limit:**

* Most textures are reduced to **1×1 pixel** → **1 pixel total**
* Some textures stay slightly larger **(up to 10×10 = 100 pixels)** so blocks remain recognizable

> ✅ Fewer pixels = less rendering work = **higher FPS** ⚡

## 🌱 Grass removal feature

> One of the biggest FPS killers in Minecraft is **grass and foliage rendering**.

> 🧹 This pack **completely removes grass textures**, making them invisible.

## ⚠️ Why isn’t the FPS boost HUGE yet?

> Minecraft has **~1,200 block textures**.

> 🛠️ Not all of them are converted yet — some textures are still default (16×16) (the progress bar shows how many textures have already been converted to potato).

> 📈 As more textures get optimized, **performance will improve with every update**.

## ❓ FAQ

**Q: Does this really increase FPS?**

> Yes — mainly on **low-end hardware**. The more optimized textures you use, the bigger the gain.

**Q: Why do some blocks still look normal?**

> Those textures **haven’t been converted yet**. This pack is still a work in progress.

**Q: Is this better than OptiFine / Sodium?**

> No replacement — it **stacks perfectly** with performance mods.
> 🔥 Best combo: **Potato Pack + Sodium/OptiFine**

**Q: Does this work on servers?**

> Yes, resource packs are **client-side only**.

**Q: Can this be considered a cheat?**

> ⚠️ **On some servers, yes.**
> Removing grass and visual clutter **may be considered an unfair advantage**.

> 👉 Use at **your own risk**, and always check server rules before playing.

**Q: Can I get this pack on bedrock?**

> No, bedrock version is not currently available.

## 🛣️ Roadmap

**Planned updates:**

* 🧱 Full potato overworld
* 🔥 Full potato nether 
* 🐉 Full potato end

## 👍 Great for:

* **Low-end** computers 🧺
* **Maximum FPS** seekers ⚡
* PvP & competitive players
* Certified potato enjoyers 🥔

*Don’t expect beauty — expect pure performance.*
*Your game has never looked this… potato.*

## 🤝 Want to help?

This pack is a work in progress, and help is always welcome!

If you know how to use Photoshop (or similar tools) and want to help convert textures into potato resolution, you can join the project.

📝 Just make potato texture and send the image here (dont forget to put name of texture):

👉 **[SEND TEXTURE](https://forms.gle/YZ696Lu7PpSzigeM6)**

🥔 Even a few textures help — every converted pixel counts.
